preamble: Fronting and palatalization of uvular nasals can occur under the influence of front vowels or as part of a general move toward the palatal region, representing a shift in articulatory focus.

examples.note: The Japanese uvular nasal /ɴ/ can be fronted to a palatal nasal /ɲ/ when followed by high front vowels or the palatal glide /j/.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
